Base directory for this skill: /home/runner/work/my-harness/my-harness/.claude/skills/jira-cli

# Jira CLI

Interact with Atlassian Jira from the command line using [jira-cli](https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli).

## When to Use

- User asks to create, view, edit, or search Jira issues/tickets
- User needs to transition issues through workflow states (To Do → In Progress → Done)
- User wants to manage sprints, epics, or boards
- User needs to assign issues, add comments, or log work time
- User asks about their current tasks or sprint progress

## Prerequisites

1. Install jira-cli: `brew install ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/jira-cli` (macOS) or download from [releases](https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/releases)
2. Set API token: `export JIRA_API_TOKEN="your-token"`
3. Initialize: `jira init` and follow prompts

## Issue Commands

### List Issues

```bash
# List issues in current project
jira issue list

# List my assigned issues
jira issue list -a$(jira me)

# List issues by status
jira issue list -s"In Progress"

# List high priority issues
jira issue list -yHigh

# List issues with multiple filters
jira issue list -a$(jira me) -s"To Do" -yHigh --created week

# List issues with raw JQL
jira issue list -q "project = PROJ AND status = 'In Progress'"

# Plain text output for scripting
jira issue list --plain --columns key,summary,status --no-headers
```

### Create Issues

```bash
# Interactive issue creation
jira issue create

# Create with all options specified
jira issue create -tBug -s"Login button not working" -b"Description here" -yHigh --no-input

# Create a story
jira issue create -tStory -s"Add user authentication" -yMedium

# Create with labels and components
jira issue create -tTask -s"Update dependencies" -lmaintenance -l"tech-debt" -Cbackend

# Create and assign to self
jira issue create -tBug -s"Fix crash on startup" -a$(jira me) --no-input
```

### View Issues

```bash
# View issue details
jira issue view ISSUE-123

# View with comments
jira issue view ISSUE-123 --comments 10

# View in plain text
jira issue view ISSUE-123 --plain

# Open issue in browser
jira open ISSUE-123
```

### Edit Issues

```bash
# Edit summary
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -s"Updated summary"

# Edit description
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -b"New description"

# Edit priority
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -yHigh

# Add labels
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -lnew-label
```

### Transition Issues

```bash
# Move issue to a new status
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "In Progress"

# Move with comment
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" --comment "Completed the task"

# Move and set resolution
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" -RFixed
```

### Assign Issues

```bash
# Assign to self
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 $(jira me)

# Assign to specific user
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 username

# Unassign
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 x
```

### Comments

```bash
# Add a comment
jira issue comment add ISSUE-123 "This is my comment"

# Add comment from editor
jira issue comment add ISSUE-123
```

### Work Logging

```bash
# Log time
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "2h 30m"

# Log time with comment
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "1d 4h" --comment "Completed feature implementation" --no-input
```

### Link & Clone Issues

```bash
# Link two issues
jira issue link ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456 Blocks

# Unlink issues
jira issue unlink ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Clone an issue
jira issue clone ISSUE-123 -s"Cloned: New summary"

# Delete an issue
jira issue delete ISSUE-123
```

## Epic Commands

```bash
# List epics
jira epic list

# List epics in table format
jira epic list --table

# Create an epic
jira epic create -n"Q1 Features" -s"Epic summary" -b"Epic description"

# Add issues to epic
jira epic add EPIC-1 ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Remove issues from epic
jira epic remove ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456
```

## Sprint Commands

```bash
# List sprints
jira sprint list

# List current/active sprint
jira sprint list --current

# List my issues in current sprint
jira sprint list --current -a$(jira me)

# Add issues to sprint
jira sprint add SPRINT_ID ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456
```

## Project & Board Commands

```bash
# List projects
jira project list

# List boards
jira board list

# List releases/versions
jira release list

# Open project in browser
jira open
```

## Utility Commands

```bash
# Get current username
jira me

# Show help
jira --help
jira issue --help

# Setup shell completion
jira completion bash  # or zsh, fish, powershell
```

## Common Flags

| Flag | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `--plain` | Plain text output (no interactive UI) |
| `--raw` | Raw JSON output |
| `--csv` | CSV output |
| `--no-input` | Skip interactive prompts |
| `-t, --type` | Issue type (Bug, Story, Task, Epic) |
| `-s, --summary` | Issue summary/title |
| `-b, --body` | Issue description |
| `-y, --priority` | Priority (Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest) |
| `-l, --label` | Labels (repeatable) |
| `-a, --assignee` | Assignee username |
| `-r, --reporter` | Reporter username |
| `-C, --component` | Component name |
| `-P, --parent` | Parent issue/epic key |
| `-q, --jql` | Raw JQL query |
| `--created` | Filter by creation date (-7d, week, month) |
| `--order-by` | Sort field |
| `--reverse` | Reverse sort order |

## Common Workflows

### Start Working on an Issue

```bash
# Assign to self and move to In Progress
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 $(jira me)
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "In Progress"
```

### Complete an Issue

```bash
# Log work and close
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "4h" --no-input
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" --comment "Completed" -RFixed
```

### Daily Standup Review

```bash
# View my current sprint tasks
jira sprint list --current -a$(jira me)
```

### Create and Track a Bug

```bash
# Create bug
jira issue create -tBug -s"App crashes on login" -yHigh -lbug --no-input
# Note the returned issue key, then assign
jira issue assign BUG-123 $(jira me)
jira issue move BUG-123 "In Progress"
```

## Output Examples

| Command | Use Case |
| ------- | -------- |
| `jira issue list --plain` | Script-friendly output |
| `jira issue list --raw` | JSON for parsing |
| `jira issue list --csv` | Export to spreadsheet |

## Limitations

- Requires prior `jira init` configuration
- Some features may vary between Jira Cloud and Server
- Complex custom fields may require `--custom` flag with field IDs
- Rate limits apply based on Jira instance configuration

  # Build PR Refinement agent (counter-voice to PR Review)

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Description                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  Create the PR Refinement agent: a daily cron job that reads inline comments posted by the PR Review agent and pushes
  back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Three files to create:                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md — modeled on agents/pr-review/prompt.md                                            
  • agents/pr-refinement/README.md — mirrors agents/pr-review/README.md in structure                                  
  • .github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml — daily cron sweep (not event-triggered); scans all open PRs that already have
  pr-review inline comments                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  Note: pr-review ( .github/workflows/pr-review.yml ) triggers on pull_request events; refinement runs its own daily  
  cron.                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  The PR Review + PR Refinement pair acts as a deliberate adversarial check: one model flags issues, a different model
  pushes back. This hedge against single-model review bias is a named design choice in docs/agentic-workflow.md       
  (Design                                                                                                             
  choices). The pr-review workflow already exists at .github/workflows/pr-review.yml ; refinement is its complement.  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Implementation notes                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  **Model choice:** Use a different Claude model than pr-review's default ( anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with no --
  model override). Specify via claude_args: --model ❬model-id❭ in the workflow. Verify current model IDs against      
  Anthropic docs and document the choice and rationale in agents/pr-refinement/README.md .                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • A PR that has pr-review inline comments receives a refinement pass that selectively pushes back on specific       
  comments.                                                                                                           
  • A PR that has reached the round cap is skipped in subsequent daily runs.                                          
  • Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-❬PR_NUMBER❭/❬RUN_ID❭.json — matching the convention in
  the "Commit execution log" step of .github/workflows/pr-review.yml .                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which stop condition should the refinement agent use to prevent re-processing the same PR indefinitely: (A)     
  store a round counter as a PR label (e.g. refinement-rounds:N ) and skip the PR once it reaches a cap, or (B) freeze
  once a human has posted on the PR?                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ 1 Comments ------------------------                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  Jae Yoon Bae • Thu, 11 Jun 26 • Latest comment                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  Yes, maybe let's try to track how many times it was refined, and then, based on that, stop. For now, I think one    
  round would be sufficient, but it should be variable.

You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

1	# PR Review agent
2	
3	Daily/per-PR reviewer. Reads the PR diff, posts inline comments with severity
4	and reasoning, optionally a short top-level summary. Comment-only — never
5	approves, merges, or pushes code.
6	
7	Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).
8	
9	## Files
10	
11	- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
12	- `../../.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.
13	
14	## How it runs
15	
16	The workflow `pr-review.yml` triggers on pull_request events
17	(`opened`, `synchronize`, `ready_for_review`, `reopened`). It checks out the
18	PR, prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to
19	`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action installs Claude Code on the
20	runner and runs a headless session in the working directory.
21	
22	`track_progress: true` is enabled — the action posts a sticky tracking
23	comment ("Claude Code is reviewing this pull request…") and updates it as
24	work progresses.
25	
26	## Manual run
27	
28	```sh
29	gh workflow run pr-review.yml
30	```
31	
32	This uses the `workflow_dispatch` trigger and runs against the default branch.
33	For a real review, push to a PR — the `pull_request` trigger fires
34	automatically.
35	
36	## Editing the prompt
37	
38	Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change to
39	the prompt takes effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.
40	
41	## What's deliberately not configured
42	
43	- No `--model` — uses the action's default.
44	- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
45	- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
46	- No write access to `contents:` — review-only by construction.
47	
48	## Required setup (one-time)
49	
50	Authenticated via Claude Pro/Max OAuth (uses your subscription quota, not
51	per-token API billing):
52	
53	1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
54	   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
55	2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
56	   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.
57	
58	Heads up: CI runs draw from the same 5-hour usage buckets as your local
59	Claude Code sessions. If you'd rather use per-token API billing, swap
60	`claude_code_oauth_token:` for `anthropic_api_key:` in the workflow and use
61	an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret instead.
62

# PR Review agent

Daily/per-PR reviewer. Reads the PR diff, posts inline comments with severity
and reasoning, optionally a short top-level summary. Comment-only — never
approves, merges, or pushes code.

Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.

## How it runs

The workflow `pr-review.yml` triggers on pull_request events
(`opened`, `synchronize`, `ready_for_review`, `reopened`). It checks out the
PR, prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to
`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action installs Claude Code on the
runner and runs a headless session in the working directory.

`track_progress: true` is enabled — the action posts a sticky tracking
comment ("Claude Code is reviewing this pull request…") and updates it as
work progresses.

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run pr-review.yml
```

This uses the `workflow_dispatch` trigger and runs against the default branch.
For a real review, push to a PR — the `pull_request` trigger fires
automatically.

## Editing the prompt

Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change to
the prompt takes effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--model` — uses the action's default.
- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
- No write access to `contents:` — review-only by construction.

## Required setup (one-time)

Authenticated via Claude Pro/Max OAuth (uses your subscription quota, not
per-token API billing):

1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.

Heads up: CI runs draw from the same 5-hour usage buckets as your local
Claude Code sessions. If you'd rather use per-token API billing, swap
`claude_code_oauth_token:` for `anthropic_api_key:` in the workflow and use
an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret instead.

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

agentic-workflow.md
# Agentic Development Workflow

> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.

The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.

## High-level flow

```
Humans create tickets
        │
        ▼
   Ticket Agent
        │
        ▼
Development Orchestrator
        │
        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
        │                          │
        │                          ▼
        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
        │                          ▲              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
        │                          │              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
        │                                         │
        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
                                  │   (headless `claude -p`, --max-turns)
                                  ▼               │
                              Draft PR ───────────┘
                          (human picks up &
                           merges, or re-runs)
```

## Human touchpoints

- **Start:** humans create tickets.
- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
  into the loop before implementing.

## Agents

### Ticket Agent
**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.

- Does **not** invent new tickets.
- Works on a daily basis. 
- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
- Creates dependencies between tickets (`blocks`, `depends on`).

### Development Orchestrator
**Goal:** decide which development agent to launch for each ticket.

- Reads the ticket.
- Decides the complexity (complex vs. easy) and routes accordingly.

### Senior Developer Agent — `/openspec:explore`
**Goal:** tackle complex tasks **with a human in the loop**.

- Uses spec-driven development.
- Requests human input before moving to implementation.
- Same runtime shape as Junior: headless `claude -p` on a GHA runner with
  `--max-turns` and a workflow timeout. The `/openspec:explore` skill and
  human-in-the-loop pauses layer on top of that base.

### Junior Developer Agent
**Goal:** tackle easy tasks **without a human in the loop**.

- Headless `claude -p` invoked directly from a GitHub Actions workflow
  (no skill wrapper). Plans straight through to execution.
- Bounded by `--max-turns` per dispatch + a workflow-level `timeout-minutes`.
- Creates a Draft PR; a human picks it up from there.

### PR Review Agent
**Goal:** daily review of updated PRs.

- Inline comments.
- Severity.
- Reasoning.

### PR Refinement Agent
**Goal:** daily refinement of the PR.

- May use a different model (e.g. CODEX).
- Counteracts the review (acts as a deliberate counter-voice to the reviewer).

## Design choices worth calling out

- **Complexity gate at the orchestrator.** Two-tier routing keeps the
  human-in-the-loop cost only on work that needs it.
- **Runtime split.** `claude-code-action` for read/triage surfaces
  (orchestrator, ticket grooming, PR review, agent-improver); raw
  `claude -p` for implementation lanes (junior-dev, senior-dev). The
  action's PR-comment plumbing is dead weight when the agent's job is to

You are the **PR Refinement agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Read the inline comments posted by the PR Review agent on this PR and push back
on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal. You are a deliberate
counter-voice — your goal is not to agree, it is to challenge comments that don't
hold up under scrutiny.

## Step 1: Gather the inline review comments

```sh
gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments \
  --jq '.[] | {id, path, line, body, user: .user.login, created_at}'
```

If there are no inline comments, post a brief top-level note and stop:

```sh
gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "PR Refinement: no inline review comments found to evaluate."
```

## Step 2: Understand the changes

- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. Read the context around each flagged line.
- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,headRefName` — PR metadata.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand the code surrounding
  flagged lines.

## Step 3: Evaluate each comment

For each inline comment, decide whether it deserves pushback. Push back when:

1. **Factually wrong** — the reviewer misread the code, misunderstood an API, or
   cited an incorrect constraint.
2. **Over-eager** — the reviewer flagged something intentional, already handled
   elsewhere, or covered by existing tests or docs.
3. **Low-signal** — the reviewer raised a style preference, naming bikeshed, or
   "have you considered…" speculation without identifying a real problem.
4. **Missing context** — the reviewer didn't account for surrounding code, tests,
   or design patterns visible elsewhere in the repo.

Do **not** push back on:
- Comments that identify genuine bugs or security issues.
- Comments that are factually correct and clearly reasoned.
- The wording or tone of a comment — focus on the technical substance only.

If a comment has partial merit, acknowledge the valid part while challenging the
overreach. Be precise about which claim you're disputing.

### Verifying external facts before pushing back

Do not push back based on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable
— library APIs, SDK versions, model names, deprecation status, CVE details. Look
it up first.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, changelogs not on Context7) — use
  `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages.

If you can't verify a fact, either drop the pushback or downgrade your reply to a
question rather than an assertion.

## Step 4: Reply to comments worth pushing back on

For each comment that deserves pushback, reply directly to its thread:

```sh
gh api "repos/${REPO}/pulls/comments/${COMMENT_ID}/replies" \
  -X POST \
  -f body="..."
```

Each reply must:
- Address the specific claim in the original comment by name or quote.
- Cite the code, test, or doc that supports your rebuttal.
- Be direct and collegial — this is a technical discussion, not a dismissal.
- Include a one-line verdict at the start: **Disagree:** / **Partially disagree:** /
  **Question:**

Do **not** reply to every comment. Silence on a comment means you found it
correct or well-reasoned — that is a valid outcome.

## Step 5: High-level summary (optional)

If there is a recurring pattern in the review comments that deserves a meta-level
observation (e.g. three comments appear to miss that this module intentionally
uses a specific pattern), post one short top-level comment:

```sh
gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."
```

If you pushed back on zero comments, post a brief top-level acknowledgement that
the review looks solid and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not
recap which comments you replied to. The runner will exit.

input.content:
# PR Refinement agent

Daily counter-voice to the PR Review agent. Reads inline comments posted by
pr-review, then pushes back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or
low-signal. Reply-only — never approves, merges, or pushes code.

Part of the adversarial review pair described in
[`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md). The design choice
is explicit: one model flags issues, a different model challenges them, hedging
against single-model review bias.

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.

## How it runs

The workflow `pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron (09:00 UTC). It scans all
open PRs, skips any that have not yet been reviewed by the PR Review agent (no
entry under `.github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-<N>/` on the PR branch), and skips
any that have already reached the refinement round cap (tracked via the
`refinement-rounds:N` label on the PR). Qualifying PRs are processed in a matrix
— each spawns a separate `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` run using
**claude-opus-4-8**.

Model choice rationale: pr-review uses the action's default model (Sonnet-class).
Refinement uses `claude-opus-4-8` — the most capable available model — so the
counter-voice has the strongest reasoning to challenge reviewer claims rather than
just echoing them. Different model tiers means different training emphases, which
increases the chance the two agents genuinely disagree where disagreement is
warranted.

`track_progress: false` is set — no sticky tracking comment is posted to the PR.

## Round cap

The number of refinement rounds per PR is controlled by the `MAX_ROUNDS`
workflow input (default: `1`). After each completed run the workflow applies a
`refinement-rounds:N` label to the PR. On the next daily sweep, any PR whose
label count equals `MAX_ROUNDS` is skipped.

To allow more rounds on a specific PR, trigger `pr-refinement.yml` manually via
`workflow_dispatch` with a higher `max_rounds` value.

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run pr-refinement.yml
```

Runs with the default `max_rounds: 1`. To override:

```sh
gh workflow run pr-refinement.yml -f max_rounds=2
```

## Editing the prompt

Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so changes take
effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
- No write access to `contents:` at the PR-review stage — reply comments only.

## Required setup (one-time)

Same OAuth setup as the PR Review agent:

1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.

The workflow also uses `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` for library fact-checking (same secret
used by pr-review).

input.content:
name: PR Refinement agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      max_rounds:
        description: 'Max refinement rounds per PR (default: 1)'
        required: false
        default: '1'
        type: string

jobs:
  find-prs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      pull-requests: read
      contents: read
    outputs:
      matrix: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.matrix }}
      has_prs: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.has_prs }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: scan
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          MAX_ROUNDS: ${{ inputs.max_rounds || '1' }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
        run: |
          INCLUDE="[]"

          while IFS= read -r PR; do
            [ -z "$PR" ] && continue

            # Only process open PRs (skip closed/merged)
            PR_STATE=$(gh pr view "$PR" --json state --jq '.state' 2>/dev/null || echo "UNKNOWN")
            [ "$PR_STATE" != "OPEN" ] && continue

            # Get PR branch name
            BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$PR" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
            [ -z "$BRANCH" ] && continue

            # Check if pr-review agent has already run on this PR
            # (presence of any log file under .github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-N/ on the PR branch)
            REVIEW_LOG_COUNT=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/contents/.github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR}" \
              --ref "$BRANCH" --jq 'length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            [ "${REVIEW_LOG_COUNT:-0}" -eq "0" ] && continue

            # Check how many refinement rounds have already been done via label
            CURRENT_LABEL=$(gh pr view "$PR" --json labels \
              --jq '.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))' 2>/dev/null \
              | head -1 || echo "")
            ROUNDS=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABEL" | sed 's/refinement-rounds://' | tr -d '[:space:]')
            ROUNDS=${ROUNDS:-0}

            if [ "${ROUNDS}" -lt "${MAX_ROUNDS}" ] 2>/dev/null; then
              INCLUDE=$(echo "$INCLUDE" | jq ". + [{\"pr\": $PR}]")
              echo "PR #${PR} qualifies (refinement round ${ROUNDS}/${MAX_ROUNDS})"
            else
              echo "PR #${PR} skipped (at round cap ${ROUNDS}/${MAX_ROUNDS})"
            fi
          done < <(gh pr list --state open --json number --jq '.[].number')

          HAS_PRS=false
          if [ "$(echo "$INCLUDE" | jq 'length')" -gt "0" ]; then
            HAS_PRS=true
          fi

          echo "matrix={\"include\": $INCLUDE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "has_prs=$HAS_PRS" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Qualifying PRs: $INCLUDE"

  refine:
    needs: find-prs
    if: needs.find-prs.outputs.has_prs == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    strategy:
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.find-prs.outputs.matrix) }}
      fail-fast: false
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${PR_NUMBER}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --model claude-opus-4-8 --allowedTools "mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh api repos/*/pulls/*/comments:*),Bash(gh api repos/*/pulls/comments/*/replies:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Update refinement-rounds label
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
          MAX_ROUNDS: ${{ inputs.max_rounds || '1' }}
        run: |
          set -e

          CURRENT_LABEL=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels \
            --jq '.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))' 2>/dev/null \
            | head -1 || echo "")
          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABEL" | sed 's/refinement-rounds://' | tr -d '[:space:]')
          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(echo "${CURRENT_ROUNDS:-0}" | grep -E '^[0-9]+$' || echo "0")
          NEW_ROUNDS=$((CURRENT_ROUNDS + 1))

          # Ensure the new label exists at the repo level
          gh label create "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}" \
            --color "e4e669" --description "Refined ${NEW_ROUNDS} time(s)" \
            --force 2>/dev/null || true

          # Swap old label for new
          if [ -n "$CURRENT_LABEL" ]; then
            gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --remove-label "$CURRENT_LABEL" 2>/dev/null || true
          fi
          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --add-label "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName')
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: pr-refinement PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

        run: |
          set -e

          CURRENT_LABEL=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels \
            --jq '.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))' 2>/dev/null \
            | head -1 || echo "")
          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABEL" | sed 's/refinement-rounds://' | tr -d '[:space:]')
          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(echo "${CURRENT_ROUNDS:-0}" | grep -E '^[0-9]+$' || echo "0")
          NEW_ROUNDS=$((CURRENT_ROUNDS + 1))

          # Ensure the new label exists at the repo level
          gh label create "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}" \
            --color "e4e669" --description "Refined ${NEW_ROUNDS} time(s)" \
            --force 2>/dev/null || true

          # Swap old label for new
          if [ -n "$CURRENT_LABEL" ]; then
            gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --remove-label "$CURRENT_LABEL" 2>/dev/null || true
          fi
          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --add-label "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName')
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: pr-refinement PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Refinement agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Read the inline comments posted by the PR Review agent on this PR and push back
8	on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal. You are a deliberate
9	counter-voice — your goal is not to agree, it is to challenge comments that don't
10	hold up under scrutiny.
11	
12	## Step 1: Gather the inline review comments
13	
14	```sh
15	gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments \
16	  --jq '.[] | {id, path, line, body, user: .user.login, created_at}'
17	```
18	
19	If there are no inline comments, post a brief top-level note and stop:
20	
21	```sh
22	gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "PR Refinement: no inline review comments found to evaluate."
23	```
24	
25	## Step 2: Understand the changes
26	
27	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. Read the context around each flagged line.
28	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,headRefName` — PR metadata.
29	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand the code surrounding
30	  flagged lines.
31	
32	## Step 3: Evaluate each comment
33	
34	For each inline comment, decide whether it deserves pushback. Push back when:
35	
36	1. **Factually wrong** — the reviewer misread the code, misunderstood an API, or
37	   cited an incorrect constraint.
38	2. **Over-eager** — the reviewer flagged something intentional, already handled
39	   elsewhere, or covered by existing tests or docs.
40	3. **Low-signal** — the reviewer raised a style preference, naming bikeshed, or
41	   "have you considered…" speculation without identifying a real problem.
42	4. **Missing context** — the reviewer didn't account for surrounding code, tests,
43	   or design patterns visible elsewhere in the repo.
44	
45	Do **not** push back on:
46	- Comments that identify genuine bugs or security issues.
47	- Comments that are factually correct and clearly reasoned.
48	- The wording or tone of a comment — focus on the technical substance only.
49	
50	If a comment has partial merit, acknowledge the valid part while challenging the
51	overreach. Be precise about which claim you're disputing.
52	
53	### Verifying external facts before pushing back
54	
55	Do not push back based on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable
56	— library APIs, SDK versions, model names, deprecation status, CVE details. Look
57	it up first.
58	
59	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
60	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
61	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
62	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, changelogs not on Context7) — use
63	  `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages.
64	
65	If you can't verify a fact, either drop the pushback or downgrade your reply to a
66	question rather than an assertion.
67	
68	## Step 4: Reply to comments worth pushing back on
69	
70	For each comment that deserves pushback, reply directly to its thread:
71	
72	```sh
73	gh api "repos/${REPO}/pulls/comments/${COMMENT_ID}/replies" \
74	  -X POST \
75	  -f body="..."
76	```
77	
78	Each reply must:
79	- Address the specific claim in the original comment by name or quote.
80	- Cite the code, test, or doc that supports your rebuttal.
81	- Be direct and collegial — this is a technical discussion, not a dismissal.
82	- Include a one-line verdict at the start: **Disagree:** / **Partially disagree:** /
83	  **Question:**
84	
85	Do **not** reply to every comment. Silence on a comment means you found it
86	correct or well-reasoned — that is a valid outcome.
87	
88	## Step 5: High-level summary (optional)
89	
90	If there is a recurring pattern in the review comments that deserves a meta-level
91	observation (e.g. three comments appear to miss that this module intentionally
92	uses a specific pattern), post one short top-level comment:
93	
94	```sh
95	gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."
96	```
97	
98	If you pushed back on zero comments, post a brief top-level acknowledgement that
99	the review looks solid and stop.
100	
101	## When you're done
102	
103	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not
104	recap which comments you replied to. The runner will exit.
105

content.content:
1	# PR Refinement agent
2	
3	Daily counter-voice to the PR Review agent. Reads inline comments posted by
4	pr-review, then pushes back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or
5	low-signal. Reply-only — never approves, merges, or pushes code.
6	
7	Part of the adversarial review pair described in
8	[`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md). The design choice
9	is explicit: one model flags issues, a different model challenges them, hedging
10	against single-model review bias.
11	
12	## Files
13	
14	- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
15	- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.
16	
17	## How it runs
18	
19	The workflow `pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron (09:00 UTC). It scans all
20	open PRs, skips any that have not yet been reviewed by the PR Review agent (no
21	entry under `.github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-<N>/` on the PR branch), and skips
22	any that have already reached the refinement round cap (tracked via the
23	`refinement-rounds:N` label on the PR). Qualifying PRs are processed in a matrix
24	— each spawns a separate `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` run using
25	**claude-opus-4-8**.
26	
27	Model choice rationale: pr-review uses the action's default model (Sonnet-class).
28	Refinement uses `claude-opus-4-8` — the most capable available model — so the
29	counter-voice has the strongest reasoning to challenge reviewer claims rather than
30	just echoing them. Different model tiers means different training emphases, which
31	increases the chance the two agents genuinely disagree where disagreement is
32	warranted.
33	
34	`track_progress: false` is set — no sticky tracking comment is posted to the PR.
35	
36	## Round cap
37	
38	The number of refinement rounds per PR is controlled by the `MAX_ROUNDS`
39	workflow input (default: `1`). After each completed run the workflow applies a
40	`refinement-rounds:N` label to the PR. On the next daily sweep, any PR whose
41	label count equals `MAX_ROUNDS` is skipped.
42	
43	To allow more rounds on a specific PR, trigger `pr-refinement.yml` manually via
44	`workflow_dispatch` with a higher `max_rounds` value.
45	
46	## Manual run
47	
48	```sh
49	gh workflow run pr-refinement.yml
50	```
51	
52	Runs with the default `max_rounds: 1`. To override:
53	
54	```sh
55	gh workflow run pr-refinement.yml -f max_rounds=2
56	```
57	
58	## Editing the prompt
59	
60	Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so changes take
61	effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.
62	
63	## What's deliberately not configured
64	
65	- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
66	- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
67	- No write access to `contents:` at the PR-review stage — reply comments only.
68	
69	## Required setup (one-time)
70	
71	Same OAuth setup as the PR Review agent:
72	
73	1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
74	   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
75	2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
76	   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.
77	
78	The workflow also uses `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` for library fact-checking (same secret
79	used by pr-review).
80

content.content:
1	name: PR Refinement agent
2	
3	on:
4	  schedule:
5	    - cron: '0 9 * * *'
6	  workflow_dispatch:
7	    inputs:
8	      max_rounds:
9	        description: 'Max refinement rounds per PR (default: 1)'
10	        required: false
11	        default: '1'
12	        type: string
13	
14	jobs:
15	  find-prs:
16	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
17	    permissions:
18	      pull-requests: read
19	      contents: read
20	    outputs:
21	      matrix: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.matrix }}
22	      has_prs: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.has_prs }}
23	    steps:
24	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
25	        with:
26	          fetch-depth: 1
27	
28	      - id: scan
29	        env:
30	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
31	          MAX_ROUNDS: ${{ inputs.max_rounds || '1' }}
32	          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
33	        run: |
34	          INCLUDE="[]"
35	
36	          while IFS= read -r PR; do
37	            [ -z "$PR" ] && continue
38	
39	            # Only process open PRs (skip closed/merged)
40	            PR_STATE=$(gh pr view "$PR" --json state --jq '.state' 2>/dev/null || echo "UNKNOWN")
41	            [ "$PR_STATE" != "OPEN" ] && continue
42	
43	            # Get PR branch name
44	            BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$PR" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
45	            [ -z "$BRANCH" ] && continue
46	
47	            # Check if pr-review agent has already run on this PR
48	            # (presence of any log file under .github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-N/ on the PR branch)
49	            REVIEW_LOG_COUNT=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/contents/.github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR}" \
50	              --ref "$BRANCH" --jq 'length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
51	            [ "${REVIEW_LOG_COUNT:-0}" -eq "0" ] && continue
52	
53	            # Check how many refinement rounds have already been done via label
54	            CURRENT_LABEL=$(gh pr view "$PR" --json labels \
55	              --jq '.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))' 2>/dev/null \
56	              | head -1 || echo "")
57	            ROUNDS=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABEL" | sed 's/refinement-rounds://' | tr -d '[:space:]')
58	            ROUNDS=${ROUNDS:-0}
59	
60	            if [ "${ROUNDS}" -lt "${MAX_ROUNDS}" ] 2>/dev/null; then
61	              INCLUDE=$(echo "$INCLUDE" | jq ". + [{\"pr\": $PR}]")
62	              echo "PR #${PR} qualifies (refinement round ${ROUNDS}/${MAX_ROUNDS})"
63	            else
64	              echo "PR #${PR} skipped (at round cap ${ROUNDS}/${MAX_ROUNDS})"
65	            fi
66	          done < <(gh pr list --state open --json number --jq '.[].number')
67	
68	          HAS_PRS=false
69	          if [ "$(echo "$INCLUDE" | jq 'length')" -gt "0" ]; then
70	            HAS_PRS=true
71	          fi
72	
73	          echo "matrix={\"include\": $INCLUDE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
74	          echo "has_prs=$HAS_PRS" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
75	          echo "Qualifying PRs: $INCLUDE"
76	
77	  refine:
78	    needs: find-prs
79	    if: needs.find-prs.outputs.has_prs == 'true'
80	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
81	    permissions:
82	      contents: write
83	      pull-requests: write
84	      id-token: write
85	      actions: write
86	      checks: read
87	      statuses: read
88	    strategy:
89	      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.find-prs.outputs.matrix) }}
90	      fail-fast: false
91	    steps:
92	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
93	        with:
94	          fetch-depth: 1
95	
96	      - id: prompt
97	        env:
98	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
99	        run: |
100	          {
101	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
102	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
103	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${PR_NUMBER}"
104	            echo
105	            cat agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md
106	            echo PROMPT_EOF
107	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
108	
109	      - id: claude
110	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
111	        with:
112	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
113	          track_progress: false
114	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
115	          settings: |
116	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
117	          claude_args: |
118	            --model claude-opus-4-8 --allowedTools "mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh api repos/*/pulls/*/comments:*),Bash(gh api repos/*/pulls/comments/*/replies:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
119	
120	      - name: Update refinement-rounds label
121	        if: always()
122	        env:
123	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
124	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
125	          MAX_ROUNDS: ${{ inputs.max_rounds || '1' }}
126	        run: |
127	          set -e
128	
129	          CURRENT_LABEL=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels \
130	            --jq '.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))' 2>/dev/null \
131	            | head -1 || echo "")
132	          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(echo "$CURRENT_LABEL" | sed 's/refinement-rounds://' | tr -d '[:space:]')
133	          CURRENT_ROUNDS=$(echo "${CURRENT_ROUNDS:-0}" | grep -E '^[0-9]+$' || echo "0")
134	          NEW_ROUNDS=$((CURRENT_ROUNDS + 1))
135	
136	          # Ensure the new label exists at the repo level
137	          gh label create "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}" \
138	            --color "e4e669" --description "Refined ${NEW_ROUNDS} time(s)" \
139	            --force 2>/dev/null || true
140	
141	          # Swap old label for new
142	          if [ -n "$CURRENT_LABEL" ]; then
143	            gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --remove-label "$CURRENT_LABEL" 2>/dev/null || true
144	          fi
145	          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --add-label "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUNDS}"
146	
147	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
148	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
149	        env:
150	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
151	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr }}
152	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
153	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
154	        run: |
155	          set -e
156	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
157	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
158	          BRANCH=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName')
159	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
160	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
161	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
162	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
163	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
164	          git add "$DEST"
165	          git commit -m "agent log: pr-refinement PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
166	          git push origin "$BRANCH"
167
